Title: Pack size exceeds available stock — buttons disabled with explanation message
Preconditions:
1. Product_1 has Pack size = 100
2. Product_1 has total available stock = 62 (across all FFCs)
3. Product_1 is visible and can be purchased
4. Track inventory = true
Section: + and - Add to Cart > Min-max, pack size validation
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Navigate to the catalog and find Product_1
2. Observe the product card: stock label, price, quantity controls
3. Click into the Product Detail Page
4. Observe quantity +/- buttons and Add to Cart button
5. Check for any user-facing message explaining why the product cannot be purchased
Expected Result:
1. Product card shows 'In stock: 62' label
2. Quantity +/- buttons are disabled (correct behavior — pack size 100 > stock 62)
3. Add to Cart button is disabled
4. A visible message/tooltip explains WHY the product cannot be purchased (e.g., 'Minimum order quantity (100) exceeds available stock (62)')
5. No console errors

Title: Min quantity exceeds available stock — purchase blocked with explanation
Preconditions:
1. Product_1 has Min quantity = 10
2. Product_1 has total available stock = 5
3. Product_1 is visible and can be purchased
4. Track inventory = true
Section: + and - Add to Cart > Min-max, pack size validation
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Navigate to the catalog and find Product_1
2. Observe stock label and quantity controls on product card
3. Open Product Detail Page
4. Observe quantity input, +/- buttons, and Add to Cart state
5. Check for explanatory message
Expected Result:
1. Stock label shows 'In stock: 5'
2. Quantity +/- buttons are disabled
3. Add to Cart button is disabled
4. A visible message explains the reason (e.g., 'Minimum order quantity (10) exceeds available stock (5)')
5. Product is NOT hidden from catalog (still browsable, just not purchasable)

Title: Effective min quantity = max(minQuantity, packSize) — UI reflects correct value
Preconditions:
1. Product_1 has Min quantity = 5 and Pack size = 100 in Admin
2. Product_1 has stock > 100
3. Product_1 is visible and can be purchased
Section: + and - Add to Cart > Min-max, pack size validation
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Open Admin > Catalog > find Product_1
2. Note Min quantity (5) and Pack size (100)
3. Open storefront > navigate to Product_1
4. Observe the default quantity in the input field
5. Try to set quantity = 5 manually and click Add to Cart
6. Set quantity = 100 and click Add to Cart
Expected Result:
1. Default quantity in input field is 100 (not 5) — effective minimum is max(5, 100) = 100
2. Entering quantity = 5 is rejected or auto-corrected to 100
3. Entering quantity = 100 succeeds — product is added to cart
4. The +/- buttons increment/decrement by pack size (100)
5. UI shows the effective minimum, not the Admin-configured min quantity

Title: Pack size product with sufficient stock — add to cart succeeds in pack increments
Preconditions:
1. Product_1 has Pack size = 24
2. Product_1 has total available stock >= 48
3. Product_1 is visible and can be purchased
Section: + and - Add to Cart > Min-max, pack size validation
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Navigate to Product_1 on storefront
2. Observe default quantity value
3. Click + button once
4. Click + button again
5. Click Add to Cart
6. Verify cart contents
Expected Result:
1. Default quantity = 24 (one pack)
2. After first +: quantity = 48 (two packs)
3. After second +: quantity = 72 (three packs) — or capped if stock < 72
4. Product added to cart with correct quantity
5. Cart line item shows quantity in pack multiples
6. Subtotal = unit price x quantity

Title: Multi-FFC stock aggregation vs pack size threshold
Preconditions:
1. Product_1 has Pack size = 100
2. FFC_1 (New York) has 55 units of Product_1
3. FFC_2 (Tennessee) has 7 units of Product_1
4. Total stock displayed = 62
Section: + and - Add to Cart > Min-max, pack size validation
Steps:
Step Description:
1. Open Admin > Inventory > check Product_1 stock across all FFCs
2. Confirm total = FFC_1 + FFC_2 = 55 + 7 = 62
3. Open storefront > navigate to Product_1
4. Verify displayed stock count
5. Verify add-to-cart is correctly disabled (pack size 100 > total stock 62)
6. In Admin, increase FFC_1 stock to 95 (total becomes 102)
7. Wait for index rebuild, refresh storefront
8. Verify add-to-cart is now enabled with default quantity = 100
Expected Result:
1. Stock label shows aggregated total (62) from all FFCs
2. With stock 62 < pack size 100: buttons disabled, explanatory message shown
3. After stock increase to 102 >= pack size 100: buttons enabled, default qty = 100
4. Product can be added to cart after stock correction
